ksas.statement: Knowledge of compliance assessment methodologies, including control testing, evidence gathering, gap analysis, and findings documentation standards.

Knowledge of major data protection regulations and their applicability criteria, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, POPI Act, and emerging state and national privacy laws, sufficient to determine which regulations apply to a given data processing activity.

Knowledge of AI-specific regulatory frameworks, including the EU AI Act risk classification system, prohibited practices, high-risk AI system requirements, conformity assessment obligations, and AI literacy mandates.

Knowledge of sector-specific data regulations and their interaction with general data protection law, including HIPAA (healthcare), GLBA and SOX (financial services), FERPA (education), and PCI DSS (payment card industry).

Knowledge of lawful bases for data processing under applicable regulations, including consent requirements, contractual necessity, legal obligations, vital interests, public task authority, and legitimate interest balancing tests.

Knowledge of data subject rights under applicable data protection regulations, including rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability, objection, and rights related to automated decision-making and profiling.

Knowledge of international data transfer mechanisms under applicable regulations, including adequacy decisions, Standard Contractual Clauses, Binding Corporate Rules, Transfer Impact Assessments, and supplementary safeguards requirements.

Knowledge of regulatory enforcement mechanisms, including supervisory authority powers, penalty structures, precedent enforcement actions, and the distinction between binding requirements, regulatory guidance, and industry codes of conduct.

Knowledge of model risk management regulatory expectations, including SR 11-7, SS1/23, and EU AI Act requirements as they apply to AI and ML models in regulated industries, including effective challenge, validation, and ongoing monitoring obligations.

Knowledge of Data Protection Impact Assessment requirements, including regulatory triggers that mandate a DPIA, mandatory assessment elements, supervisory authority consultation obligations, and documentation standards.

Knowledge of data breach notification requirements across applicable jurisdictions, including notification timelines, content requirements, authority versus data subject notification obligations, and breach severity assessment criteria.

Knowledge of data retention and disposal requirements under applicable regulations, including statutory retention periods, legal hold obligations, defensible deletion practices, and the interaction between retention requirements and erasure rights.

Knowledge of children's data protection requirements, including COPPA, the Age Appropriate Design Code, and enhanced consent and processing restrictions that apply when data subjects are minors under applicable jurisdictions.

Knowledge of regulatory examination and audit processes, including common examination scopes, documentation expectations, evidence standards, examiner interaction protocols, and typical findings for data and AI regulatory reviews.
Skill in conducting data compliance gap assessments against specific regulatory frameworks, producing findings with evidence references and prioritized remediation recommendations.
Skill in interpreting regulatory text, supervisory guidance, and enforcement decisions, translating legal requirements into operational controls and measurable organizational obligations.

Skill in conducting cross-jurisdictional compliance mapping, identifying conflicts-of-law and overlapping requirements, and developing harmonized compliance approaches that satisfy multiple regulatory regimes simultaneously.

Skill in conducting Data Protection Impact Assessments, including systematic risk identification for data processing activities, proportionality analysis, mitigation measure design, and documentation to regulatory standards.

Skill in regulatory horizon scanning, including monitoring legislative developments across relevant jurisdictions, assessing organizational impact of proposed and enacted regulations, and planning compliance adaptation timelines.

Skill in preparing regulatory examination evidence packages, including control narratives, policy documentation, audit trails, and supporting artifacts that demonstrate compliance to examiner standards.

Skill in designing consent management processes that satisfy applicable regulatory requirements, including granular consent collection, preference management, withdrawal mechanisms, and audit trail maintenance.

Skill in conducting AI conformity assessments under the EU AI Act, including risk classification determination, technical documentation evaluation, and compliance assessment against applicable high-risk AI system requirements.

Skill in developing regulatory compliance training programs tailored to different organizational roles and competency levels, including content design, delivery method selection, and effectiveness measurement.
Skill in drafting and reviewing data processing agreements, including controller-processor terms, sub-processor obligations, cross-border transfer provisions, and regulatory compliance clauses.

Design, build, and validate clinical trial databases in electronic data capture systems, including CRF specifications, edit check logic, user acceptance testing, and documentation of compliance with study protocol and applicable regulatory requirements.
Author, maintain, and enforce the enterprise data policy library, ensuring policies are current, accessible, enforced, and aligned with regulatory requirements.
Conduct periodic data compliance assessments, documenting findings, evidence, risk ratings, and remediation assignments.
Manage regulatory data reporting obligations, including data submission preparation, accuracy verification, submission execution, and confirmation documentation.
Track data compliance remediation activities, including milestone monitoring, blocker escalation, and closure verification with evidence.

Maintain Records of Processing Activities, including processing purposes, data categories, recipient categories, transfer records, retention periods, and technical and organizational security measures descriptions.

Execute data subject rights requests within regulatory timelines, including identity verification, request scope determination, response preparation, exemption assessment, and fulfillment documentation.

Monitor and analyze regulatory developments, enforcement actions, and supervisory authority guidance relevant to organizational data and AI activities, producing impact assessments and recommended responses.

Conduct Transfer Impact Assessments for international data transfers, evaluating destination country legal frameworks against originating jurisdiction requirements and documenting supplementary safeguards where required.

Prepare and submit regulatory filings, registrations, and notifications required by applicable data protection and AI regulations, including processing registrations, DPO appointments, and AI system registrations.
Coordinate regulatory examination responses, including information request fulfillment, examiner access management, findings response preparation, and corrective action plan development.
Conduct AI system risk classification under the EU AI Act, documenting risk category determination, applicable requirements identification, and conformity assessment pathway selection.
Manage the consent lifecycle across organizational systems, including consent collection configuration, preference storage, withdrawal processing, re-consent campaigns, and audit record maintenance.
Ability to translate complex regulatory requirements into clear, actionable organizational policies and operational procedures that non-legal staff can implement and verify.

Ability to assess organizational compliance posture across multiple jurisdictions simultaneously, identifying coverage gaps, requirement conflicts, and opportunities for harmonized compliance approaches.
Ability to communicate regulatory risk and compliance status to executive and board-level audiences, translating legal and technical compliance details into business impact language.
Ability to adapt compliance programs to evolving regulatory landscapes, maintaining organizational readiness as new regulations emerge, existing requirements change, or enforcement priorities shift.
Ability to evaluate the regulatory implications of proposed data processing activities, AI system deployments, or business model changes, providing compliance risk assessment before implementation.
